_phase_4_b5_note: text_model_passd (Phase 4 B5 decorrelation): the model the Stage 4 text RUBRIC uses. Set it to a DIFFERENT family than text_model (e.g. 'google/gemma-4-12b-qat' when Pass B runs qwen) so the rubric's errors decorrelate from Pass B's — an independent second opinion, not a correlated echo. null (default) => falls back to text_model_passb => text_model (no decorrelation, no behavior change). VRAM: LM Studio pools both GPUs (5060 Ti 16 GB + 6700 XT 12 GB ~= 28 GB Vulkan). gemma-4-12b (~7 GB) + qwen-35b (~22 GB) ~= 29 GB just exceeds the pool, so co-loading is marginal (LM Studio swaps/partially spills around the rubric call); pair gemma-4 with a SMALLER text_model_passb to fit both cleanly. The 16 GB figure is only the CUDA/Whisper ceiling (NVIDIA card alone), NOT the LM Studio budget.
_phase_5_note: Phase 5.1 split: `text_model_passb` and `vision_model_stage6` optionally override `text_model` / `vision_model` for those specific stages. When null (the default), they fall back to the unified model. Stage 3 segment classification always uses `text_model` regardless. Recommended split on a 48 GB rig: text_model_passb = 'qwen/qwen3-32b' (text-only, non-thinking, ~28 GB BF16, ~3x cheaper than multimodal for Pass B's classification workload), vision_model_stage6 = 'qwen/qwen3-vl-8b' (vision-specialist, ~10 GB FP8, stronger on Twitch OCR). The two can be co-resident; no hot-swap needed.
